classes.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.system.RequestMetadata
documentation:
Metadata about the request. Used for tracking and abuse prevention.

Examples:
    >>> metadata = RequestMetadata(user_id="a1b2c3d4")

classes.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.system.SystemPromptContent
documentation:
Model for structured system prompt content.

Examples:
    >>> system = SystemPromptContent(
    ...     type="text",
    ...     text="You are a helpful assistant",
    ...     cache_control=CacheControl(type="ephemeral")
    ... )

classes.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.message.Role
documentation: Role enumeration for message roles.

classes.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.message.Message
documentation:
Model for a message in the conversation.

A message can contain either a string or a list of content blocks.

classes.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.request_body.AnthropicMessageRequestBody

methods.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.request_body.AnthropicMessageRequestBody.validate_temperature_and_top_p
documentation: Validate that temperature and top_p are not both set.

methods.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.request_body.AnthropicMessageRequestBody.validate_tools_and_tool_choice
documentation: Validate that tool_choice is only set when tools are provided.

Model for creating a message request to the Anthropic API.

This model represents all possible parameters that can be sent in a message request.
Messages API operates on alternating user and assistant conversational turns.

The model will generate the next Message in the conversation based on the provided messages.
Consecutive user or assistant turns in your request will be combined into a single turn.

classes.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.responses.events.ErrorType
documentation: Types of errors that can occur in streaming.

classes.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.responses.events.StreamError
documentation:
Model for streaming error events.

Examples:
    >>> error = StreamError(
    ...     type="error",
    ...     error={"type": "overloaded_error", "message": "Overloaded"}
    ... )

classes.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.responses.events.ContentBlockStop

classes.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.responses.events.MessageStop

classes.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.responses.events.PingEvent

classes.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.responses.events.MessageStartEvent

methods.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.responses.events.MessageStartEvent.validate_message
documentation: Validate that the message field can be converted to AnthropicMessageResponseBody.

Model for the message_start event.

This event contains a Message object with empty content.

classes.name: lion_anthropic.anthropic_model.AnthropicModel

methods.name: lion_anthropic.anthropic_model.AnthropicModel.stream
documentation: Handle streaming response from the model.

methods.name: lion_anthropic.anthropic_model.AnthropicModel.invoke
documentation:
Invoke the model with the given parameters.
Handles both streaming and non-streaming responses.

Model class for Anthropic API interactions.
Handles request management, rate limiting, and token calculation.

classes.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.responses.types.StopReason
documentation:
Enumeration of possible reasons why the model stopped generating.

Attributes:
    END_TURN: the model reached a natural stopping point
    MAX_TOKENS: exceeded the requested max_tokens or the model's maximum
    STOP_SEQUENCE: one of the provided custom stop_sequences was generated
    TOOL_USE: the model invoked one or more tools

classes.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.responses.stream_events.MetadataEvent
documentation: Model for metadata events containing message information.

classes.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.responses.stream_events.StreamEvent

methods.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.responses.stream_events.StreamEvent.model_validate
documentation: Validate and convert raw data into appropriate event type.

Union of all possible streaming event types.

This model discriminates based on the 'type' field to determine which
specific event model to use.

classes.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.responses.delta.TextDelta
documentation: Model for text deltas in content block updates.

classes.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.responses.delta.InputJsonDelta
documentation: Model for input JSON deltas in content block updates.

classes.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.responses.delta.ContentBlockStart

classes.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.responses.delta.ContentBlockDelta
documentation:
Model for content_block_delta events.

These events contain updates to specific content blocks identified by their index.

classes.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.responses.content.MessageResponseContent
documentation:
Base model for message response content blocks.
Each content block must have a type field.

classes.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.responses.content.TextResponseContent
documentation:
Model for text content in responses.

Examples:
    >>> text_content = TextResponseContent(
    ...     type="text",
    ...     text="Hi, I'm Claude."
    ... )

classes.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.responses.content.ToolUseResponseContent
documentation:
Model for tool use content in responses.

Examples:
    >>> tool_use = ToolUseResponseContent(
    ...     type="tool_use",
    ...     id="toolu_01D7FLrfh4GYq7yT1ULFeyMV",
    ...     name="get_stock_price",
    ...     input={"ticker": "AAPL"}
    ... )

classes.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.responses.usage.Usage

methods.name: lion_anthropic.api_endpoints.messages.responses.usage.Usage.from_dict
documentation: Create a Usage object from a dictionary.

Model for token usage information in the response.

Anthropic's API bills and rate-limits by token counts. Token counts in usage will not
match one-to-one with the exact visible content of an API request or response due to
internal transformations and parsing.

Examples:
    >>> usage = Usage(
    ...     input_tokens=50,
    ...     output_tokens=100,
    ...     cache_creation_input_tokens=None,
    ...     cache_read_input_tokens=None
    ... )
